head.hi: The Acting Secretary of State to Certain Diplomatic and Consular Officers
head.note: The diplomatic and consular officers in Ankara (for repetition to Istanbul), Bern, Lisbon, Madrid, Rome, and Stockholm.

div.p: With reference to relations with Bulgarian diplomatic representatives, you should be guided by the directives set forth in Department’s circular telegram of October 4, 9 p.m.,Vol. iv, section under Rumania entitled “Post-armistice problems of occupation …” regarding relations with Rumanian representatives.

In addition to American representation on Allied Control Commissions which are being established in Bulgaria and Rumania pursuant to Armistice terms,For text of the armistice agreement with Rumania, signed at Moscow, September 13, 1944, at 5 a.m., see Department of State Executive Agreement Series No. 490, or 59 Stat. (pt. 2) 1712. For correspondence regarding negotiations, see vol. iv, section under Rumania entitled “Negotiations leading to signing of armistice with Rumania …” American Missions are proceeding to Sofia and Bucharest headed by Foreign Service Officers Maynard Barnes and Burton Berry, respectively, who have the personal rank of Minister.

Although the establishment of these Missions does not signify the resumption of formal diplomatic relations with the countries in question, it is expected that the Missions will have informal relations with
the local authorities and such facilities and freedom of movement as may be necessary to enable them to fulfill their responsibilities for the protection of American interests.
